title: thymus polytrichus subsp. ligusticus
intro: Thymus polytrichus subsp. ligusticus, also known as the Ligurian thyme, is a subspecies of the thyme plant, a low-growing, evergreen herb native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe sore throats, coughs, and digestive issues. The Ligurian thyme is also known for its ability to boost the immune system and reduce stress, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds, flu, and anxiety. Ligurian thyme can be consumed as a tea, added to soups and stews, or used in herbal preparations such as essential oils and tinctures.
